question_html:
Write down the appropriate preposition for the following sentences:

(a) She has recovered ____ her illness.

(b) His sympathy ____ the poor is praiseworthy.

(c) I was quite unaware _____ the problem.

(d) Mehrab works ______ a law firm.

(e) He is completely void _____ charm so far as I can see.
solution_html:
(a) from (b) for (c) of (d) for (e) of

Marks Distribution:

For writing the appropriate prepositions; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:
‎come across, give in, take after, run out, bring in.
solution_html:
(a) I came across an interesting article while browsing the internet.
‎(b) She refused to give in to their demands.
‎(c) He really takes after his father in both looks and mannerisms.
‎(d) We have run out of milk and need to buy more.
‎(e) The government plans to bring in new laws to protect the environment.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html: Write a paragraph on “The Importance of Renewable Energy”.
solution_html:
The Importance of Renewable Energy

The global shift towards renewable energy is critical for sustainable development. Fossil fuels, while a major energy source, contribute heavily to greenhouse gas emissions, exacerbating global warming. Renewable energy sources like solar, wind, and hydropower provide cleaner alternatives. For a developing country like Bangladesh, renewable energy presents an opportunity to address power shortages sustainably. Initiatives like solar home systems in rural areas and wind power projects in coastal regions highlight the potential of renewable energy to transform lives and reduce dependence on fossil fuel.

Marks Distribution:

For writing Title, “The Importance of Renewable Energy”; 0.5 mark.

For writing the importance of renewable energy; 1.5 marks

For mentioning the renewable energy sources; 1.5 marks.

For mentioning the relevance for developing countries; 01 mark.

For specific initiatives in Bangladesh; 0.5 mark.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
